name: Chicken Chow Mein
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Dried or fresh egg noodles
2 tsp Rice wine or dry sherry
1 oz Smithfield ham or prosciutto, finely shredded
recipeInstructions: If you're using dried noodles, cook according to package instructions, then cool them in cold water until ready to use. If using fresh Chinese noodles, boil for 3-to-5 minutes, then immerse in cold water. Slice chicken into shreds about 2 inches long. Mix with 2 teaspoons of light soy sauce and rice wine or sherry in a small bowl; stir well. Let marinate at room temperature for 10 minutes. Heat a wok or large skillet. Add oil, then chicken shreds. Stir-fry for 2 minutes, transfer to a plate. Clean the wok. Drain noodles, removing as much water as possible. Reheat pan, add oil and garlic. Stir-fry for 10 seconds, then add snow peas and ham. Stir-fry for 1 minute, then add noodles, sugar, soy sauce, and scallions. Continue stir-frying for 2 minutes. Return chicken to noodle mixture. Continue stir-frying for 3 to 4 minutes or until chicken is cooked. Add sesame oil and give the mixture a few final stirs. Serve immediately on a warm platter.
